title: elaeocarpus longifolius
intro: Elaeocarpus longifolius, also known as the long-leaved blueberry tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae. The plant is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Elaeocarpus longifolius is said to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and protecting against cardiovascular disease. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically prepared by steeping the leaves and fruits in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
